items.question: Is a clear base / consolidation pattern visible (min 3 weeks)?
Is the pivot point (breakout level) clearly defined?
Is the handle tight and forming in the upper half of the base?
Is price breaking out above the pivot on today's candle?
Is breakout volume 40%+ above the 50-day average? (check on TradingView)
Is the S&P 500 in a confirmed uptrend (above 50-day MA)?
Is the stock's Relative Strength line at or near new highs?
Is the stock within 5% of its 52-week high?
Is the sector ETF in an uptrend (above 50-day MA)?
No earnings report within the next 3 weeks?
Did the stock recently break out above a clear resistance / pivot on strong volume?
Is price now pulling back to retest that breakout level (former resistance → support)?
Is the pullback on lighter volume than the breakout day (no heavy selling)?
Is the retest holding — price staying above the breakout level, not closing back inside the base?
Bullish reversal candle at the retest level (hammer / engulfing / doji + green)?
Is volume expanding on the bounce off the retest? (check on TradingView)
Is the S&P 500 in a confirmed uptrend (above 50-day MA)?
Is the stock's Relative Strength line at or near new highs?
Is the sector ETF in an uptrend (above 50-day MA)?
No earnings report within the next 3 weeks?
SPY is trading above a rising 200 SMA.
The stock is trading above a rising 200 SMA.
Momentum stack: Price > 50 SMA > 200 SMA (50 SMA rising).
Extension limit: price is relatively close to the 50 SMA (not parabolic).
Relative Strength: clear RS vs. the SPY.
Ascending support: uptrend line of higher lows with 3+ touchpoints (or forming 3rd).
Descending resistance: downtrend line of lower highs with 2-3 touchpoints.
The 5% Rule: entry (just above the downtrend line) is max 5% from the ascending support.
RVOL at breakout is > 1.5 (50%+ above average pace for the time of day).
SPY is trading above a rising 200 SMA.
The stock is trading above a rising 200 SMA.
The stock is trading above a rising 50 SMA.
Relative Strength: resilience or uptrend in RS vs. the SPY.
Ascending support: established uptrend line with exactly 2 prior touchpoints.
The setup: price is pulling back to touch the line for the 3rd time.
Volume dry-up: selling volume during the pullback is noticeably below average.
The 5% Rule: stop just below the trend line keeps total distance under 5%.
Candle shape: clear bullish reversal candle on the line (hammer / engulfing / doji+green).
RVOL on the bounce day is > 1.5 (buyers aggressively defending the line).
Reversal in trend: the stock has been declining over the past week/month — a falling stock is the positive sign, since we want the bounce up.
Reversal candle present (doji, bullish harami, hammer, marubozu, bullish engulfing, etc.).
Selling volume is drying up into the decline — exhaustion that signals a turn.
Stock is stretched below its 20-day MA (the MA acts as an upward magnet), or the stock is being supported by the 20-day MA.
Unfilled gap(s) above or below acting as a magnet to pull price.
CCI oversold (below −100) and starting to curl back up.
The reversal candle is forming on a support / trendline / Fibonacci level.
